meta_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity project template to Solve no version control (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no version control. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Antigravity Template: Solve Version Control for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop losing code. Use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to master version control and ship production-grade apps faster as a freelancer.
content.answer_block: The 'no version control' trap is a silent career killer for freelancers. By implementing the Antigravity project template, you establish an immutable architecture that forces versioning at the file level. Combined with Claude Code, you automate commit hygiene, ensuring every client deliverable is fully traceable, rollback-ready, and professional-grade.
content.intro:
The midnight sweat of a corrupted production build is a familiar ghost to every freelancer. You’ve been there: a quick hotfix turns into a catastrophic rollback failure because you were flying without a safety net. The 'it worked on my machine' excuse is the fastest way to lose a premium client and your professional reputation.

But the era of manual, error-prone deployment is over. By adopting the Antigravity project template, you move from chaotic file-shuffling to a deterministic, version-controlled workflow. This isn't just about saving code; it’s about engineering a system where every iteration is documented, versioned, and instantly recoverable.

steps.title: Initialize Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Deploy the base template to enforce strict directory structure and auto-git initialization from the first commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity standard scaffold with integrated Git hooks and pre-configured environment validation.

steps.title: Automate Versioning Logic
steps.description: Configure the project to automatically increment semantic versions on every successful build to ensure client transparency.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure auto-incrementing semantic versioning for this project, tied to the build pipeline and triggered by git tags.

steps.title: Establish Atomic Commit Workflow
steps.description: Use Claude Code to enforce atomic commits, ensuring that every push contains only one logical change.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up a git pre-commit hook that validates commit messages against the Conventional Commits specification.

steps.title: Implement State Snapshots
steps.description: Create automated checkpoints before any major feature merge to eliminate the risk of irreversible regressions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a script that generates a compressed state snapshot of the current project directory before merging feature branches.

steps.title: Audit and Deploy
steps.description: Perform a final security and version integrity scan before handing off the repository to the client.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Run an integrity audit on the repository to ensure all version tags are linked correctly to recent code changes.

pro_tips: Always use a .env.example file; never let your secrets touch the version control history.
Alias your git commands to include '--no-verify' sparingly, only for emergency hotfixes.
Tag your production releases with the client's name or project milestone for easy navigation in GitHub.
Integrate a 'dry-run' step in your Claude Code workflow to catch logic errors before they hit the main branch.
Use git-lfs for any assets or heavy AI model weights to keep your repository lean and fast.
resources: Git Best Practices for Freelancers: https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2
content.next_steps: Apply this template to your current active project to immediately retroactively version your existing codebase.
content.conversion_block: Stop treating your code as disposable. Master the Antigravity workflow and command higher rates by delivering bulletproof, versioned projects every time. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
